factors.summary: The company is actively involved in environmental and social initiatives, which are integral to its strategic direction and value proposition.

evidence.text: As a purpose-led company, we know we have a pivotal role to play across the entire Climate Response value chain – focusing on end-to-end solutions in energy transition, decarbonization, adaptation and resilience, and regenerative and nature-based climate solutions.
evidence.subsection: Climate Response

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company's active involvement in environmental and social initiatives is a significant positive development.

factors.summary: The company is heavily investing in data and digital solutions to enhance client operations and drive innovation.

evidence.text: We are harnessing our data and digital capabilities, products and tools to help our clients operate more efficiently in a safe environment and capitalize on their data more than ever before. We invest in big data, technology-enhanced and artificial intelligence solutions to help clients find better, safer and more agile ways of working.
evidence.subsection: Data Solutions

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Investing in data and digital solutions indicates a strategic move for growth and innovation.

factors.summary: The company is expanding its consulting and advisory services through strategic partnerships, aiming to provide comprehensive solutions to clients' challenges.

evidence.text: Together with our visionary partner, PA Consulting, we're expanding our position in high-end advisory services and deploying our collective strengths to create significant opportunities for our clients to adapt, innovate and transform.
evidence.subsection: Consulting and Advisory

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expansion through strategic partnerships enhances the company's advisory services and client solutions.

factors.summary: The company operates in two main segments: Infrastructure & Advanced Facilities and PA Consulting, each contributing significantly to its business strategy.

evidence.text: The services we provide to our markets fall into the following two operating segments: 1) Infrastructure & Advanced Facilities and 2) our majority investment in PA Consulting. For additional information regarding our segments, including information about our financial results by segment and financial results by geography, see Note 19- Segment Information and Note 5- Revenue Accounting for Contracts of Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements.
evidence.subsection: Operating Segments

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company's structure and segments are stable but do not indicate a clear positive or negative trend.

factors.summary: The Infrastructure & Advanced Facilities segment is focused on delivering end-to-end solutions in key areas such as climate change, energy transition, and infrastructure development, leveraging data science and technology.

evidence.text: Jacobs' Infrastructure & Advanced Facilities line of business provides end-to-end solutions for our clients’ most complex challenges related to climate change, energy transition, connected mobility, buildings and infrastructure, integrated water management and biopharmaceutical manufacturing. In doing so, we combine deep experience in the following end markets - Critical Infrastructure, Water & Environmental and Life Sciences & Advanced Manufacturing. Our core skills revolve around consulting, planning, architecture, design, engineering, infrastructure delivery services including project, program and construction management and long-term operation of facilities. Solutions are delivered as standalone professional service engagements, comprehensive program management partnerships, and selective progressive design-build and construction management at-risk delivery services. Increasingly, we use data science and technology-enabled expertise to deliver positive and enduring outcomes for our clients and communities.
evidence.subsection: Infrastructure & Advanced Facilities (I&AF)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Focus on key areas like climate change and energy transition suggests strategic growth opportunities.

factors.summary: The company's investment in PA Consulting is a strategic move to enhance its advisory capabilities, with a strong focus on innovation and sustainability.

evidence.text: Jacobs invested in a 65% stake in PA Consulting, the global innovation and transformation consultancy firm. PA Consulting accelerates new growth ideas from concept, through design and development and to commercial success, and revitalizes organizations, building leadership, culture, systems and processes to make innovation a reality.
evidence.subsection: PA Consulting

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Investment in PA Consulting enhances advisory capabilities, aligning with innovation and sustainability goals.

factors.summary: The company has completed a separation transaction involving its Critical Mission Solutions and Cyber & Intelligence businesses, resulting in a new independent entity, Amentum.

evidence.text: On September 27, 2024, Jacobs Solutions Inc. ('Jacobs') completed the previously announced Reverse Morris Trust transaction pursuant to which (i) Jacobs first transferred its Critical Mission Solutions business ('CMS') and portions of the Divergent Solutions ('DVS') business (referred to herein as the Cyber & Intelligence business ('C&I') and together with CMS referred to as the 'SpinCo Business'), to Amazon Holdco Inc., a Delaware corporation, which has been renamed Amentum Holdings, Inc. ('SpinCo') (the 'Separation'), (ii) Jacobs then effectuated a spin-off of SpinCo by distributing 124,084,108 shares of SpinCo common stock, par value $0.01 per share (the 'SpinCo Common Stock'), by way of a pro rata distribution to its shareholders such that each holder of shares of Jacobs common stock, par value $1.00 per share (the 'Jacobs Common Stock') was entitled to receive one share of SpinCo Common Stock for each share of Jacobs Common Stock held as of the record date, September 23, 2024 (the 'Distribution'), and (iii) finally, Amentum Parent Holdings LLC merged with and into SpinCo, with SpinCo surviving the merger (the 'Merger' and together with the Separation and the Distribution, the 'Separation Transaction'). The surviving entity of the Separation Transaction is now an independent public company with common stock listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol 'AMTM' ('Amentum').
evidence.subsection: Separation of Critical Mission Solutions (CMS) and Cyber & Intelligence (C&I)

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Separation of CMS and C&I businesses into Amentum is a restructuring move without clear positive or negative impact on the remaining company.

factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, particularly in the Infrastructure & Advanced Facilities (I&AF) segment, driven by higher revenues in water, life sciences, transportation, and energy sectors.

evidence.text: Revenues for the I&AF segment for the first fiscal quarter of 2025 was $2.63 billion, an increase of $122.0 million, or 4.9%, from reported amount of $2.50 billion for the corresponding period last year. The increase in revenue for the first fiscal quarter of 2025 was driven by net revenue growth across all sectors, particularly in water, life sciences, transportation and energy.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations for the three months ended December 27, 2024 and December 29, 2023

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Acceleration in demand across key sectors supports positive revenue growth and business momentum.

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, particularly in the Infrastructure & Advanced Facilities segment, which saw an increase of $3.45 billion year-over-year.

evidence.text: Backlog represents revenue we expect to realize for work to be completed by our consolidated subsidiaries and our proportionate share of work to be performed by unconsolidated joint ventures. [...] The increase in backlog in I&AF from December 29, 2023 was predominantly driven by growth across Water, Life Sciences and Transportation and Cities & Place markets.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations for the three months ended December 27, 2024 and December 29, 2023

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The increasing backlog in key segments indicates strong demand and future revenue potential.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, with the Infrastructure & Advanced Facilities segment leading the growth at 4.9% year-over-year, and PA Consulting showing slight growth.

evidence.text: Revenues for the I&AF segment for the first fiscal quarter of 2025 was $2.63 billion, an increase of $122.0 million, or 4.9%, from reported amount of $2.50 billion for the corresponding period last year. [...] Revenues for the PA Consulting segment for the first fiscal quarter of 2025 were $306.7 million, an increase of $0.7 million, or 0.2% from $306.0 million in the corresponding period last year.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations for the three months ended December 27, 2024 and December 29, 2023

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Acceleration in organic growth, especially in the I&AF segment, suggests strong business performance.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, as the Infrastructure & Advanced Facilities segment, which has higher margins, contributed a larger portion of revenue growth.

evidence.text: Gross profit for the first fiscal quarter of 2025 was $721.3 million, an increase of $56.5 million, or 8.5%, from $664.7 million from the corresponding period last year, with gross profit margins of 24.6% and 23.7% for the respective periods. The Company's increase in gross profit was mainly attributable to higher revenues as mentioned above, with favorable margin impacts from year over year mix as well as personnel cost impacts.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations for the three months ended December 27, 2024 and December 29, 2023

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards higher-margin segments improves overall profitability and margin expansion.

factors.summary: Unit volumes or transaction counts are growing, particularly in the Infrastructure & Advanced Facilities segment, which saw a 4.9% increase in revenues.

evidence.text: Revenues for the I&AF segment for the first fiscal quarter of 2025 was $2.63 billion, an increase of $122.0 million, or 4.9%, from reported amount of $2.50 billion for the corresponding period last year. The increase in revenue for the first fiscal quarter of 2025 was driven by net revenue growth across all sectors, particularly in water, life sciences, transportation and energy.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations for the three months ended December 27, 2024 and December 29, 2023

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growth in unit volumes across key sectors supports revenue expansion and market momentum.

factors.summary: The fastest-growing end markets are water, life sciences, transportation, and energy within the Infrastructure & Advanced Facilities segment, while public sector work in the PA Consulting segment is showing slower growth.

evidence.text: The increase in revenue for the first fiscal quarter of 2025 was driven by net revenue growth across all sectors, particularly in water, life sciences, transportation and energy. [...] The three month increase is primarily due to foreign currency translation which had an approximately $9.5 million in favorable impacts on revenues for the first fiscal quarter of 2025, as compared to $16.7 million in favorable impacts in the corresponding prior year period, offset by lower performance in public sector work compared to the prior year.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations for the three months ended December 27, 2024 and December 29, 2023

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong growth in key end markets indicates positive demand trends and business opportunities.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with restructuring initiatives leading to expected annualized pre-tax cash savings of $120 million to $147 million.

evidence.text: The Company incurred approximately $0.7 million during the first fiscal quarter of 2025 and $42.0 million and $17.5 million in fiscal 2024 and fiscal 2023, respectively, in pre-tax cash charges in connection with these initiatives. These actions, which are expected to be substantially completed before the end of fiscal 2025, are expected to result in estimated gross annualized pre-tax cash savings of approximately $120 million to $147 million.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations for the three months ended December 27, 2024 and December 29, 2023

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While labor costs are manageable, ongoing restructuring may have mixed effects on future costs.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding, driven by higher revenues and favorable margin impacts from segment mix.

evidence.text: Gross profit for the first fiscal quarter of 2025 was $721.3 million, an increase of $56.5 million, or 8.5%, from $664.7 million from the corresponding period last year, with gross profit margins of 24.6% and 23.7% for the respective periods. The Company's increase in gross profit was mainly attributable to higher revenues as mentioned above, with favorable margin impacts from year over year mix as well as personnel cost impacts.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations for the three months ended December 27, 2024 and December 29, 2023

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving margins due to higher revenues and favorable segment mix indicate strong financial health.

factors.summary: Restructuring and cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, with expected annualized pre-tax cash savings of $120 million to $147 million.

evidence.text: These actions, which are expected to be substantially completed before the end of fiscal 2025, are expected to result in estimated gross annualized pre-tax cash savings of approximately $120 million to $147 million. We will likely incur additional charges under this program through fiscal 2025, which are expected to result in additional savings in future periods.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations for the three months ended December 27, 2024 and December 29, 2023

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant savings from restructuring programs contribute to cost efficiency and profitability.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, particularly in the PA Consulting segment, which saw a 22.6% increase in operating profit due to cost reduction programs.

evidence.text: Operating profit for the segment for the first fiscal quarter of 2025 was $66.7 million, an increase of $12.3 million, or 22.6% from $54.5 million in the corresponding period last year. The year over year improvement in the quarter is mainly attributable to favorable impacts from cost reduction programs implemented in the prior year.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations for the three months ended December 27, 2024 and December 29, 2023

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity has significantly improved with a 22.6% increase in operating profit.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious due to the net loss attributable to continuing operations and the impact of restructuring charges, but confident in liquidity and capital resources.

evidence.text: We believe we have adequate liquidity and capital resources to fund our projected cash requirements for the next twelve months based on the liquidity provided by our cash and cash equivalents on hand, our borrowing capacity and our continuing cash from operations. We were in compliance with all of our debt covenants at December 27, 2024.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations for the three months ended December 27, 2024 and December 29, 2023

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Management shows mixed signals with cautiousness due to losses but confidence in liquidity.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include restructuring charges and the impact of mark-to-market losses on the Amentum investment, which are expected to weigh on results.

evidence.text: Included in the Company’s operating results from continuing operations for the first fiscal quarter of 2025 were $145.2 million in pre-tax fair value losses recorded in miscellaneous (expense) income, net, associated with our investment held in Amentum stock after finalization of the Separation Transaction as well as pre-tax Restructuring and other charges and transaction costs of $16.1 million associated primarily to expenses incurred relating to the Separation Transaction (primarily professional services and employee separation costs).
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations for the three months ended December 27, 2024 and December 29, 2023

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include restructuring charges and mark-to-market losses impacting results.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign currency translation impacts, which have a significant effect on both revenues and expenses.

evidence.text: Foreign currency translation had approximately $6.9 million in favorable impacts on revenues for the three months ended first fiscal quarter of 2025, as compared to $20.9 million in favorable impacts in the corresponding prior year period. [...] Unfavorable foreign exchange impacts of $3.2 million for the first fiscal quarter of 2025 as compared to unfavorable impacts of $3.6 million for the first fiscal quarter of 2024.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations for the three months ended December 27, 2024 and December 29, 2023

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Foreign currency impacts are present but not clearly positive or negative in trend.

factors.summary: A key catalyst is the expected completion of restructuring initiatives by the end of fiscal 2025, which are expected to deliver annualized pre-tax cash savings of $120 million to $147 million.

evidence.text: These actions, which are expected to be substantially completed before the end of fiscal 2025, are expected to result in estimated gross annualized pre-tax cash savings of approximately $120 million to $147 million. We will likely incur additional charges under this program through fiscal 2025, which are expected to result in additional savings in future periods.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations for the three months ended December 27, 2024 and December 29, 2023

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expected completion of restructuring initiatives will deliver significant cash savings.

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategic direction focused on sustainability and innovation, supported by significant investments in key growth areas. The strategy includes three growth accelerators: Climate Response, Data Solutions, and Consulting & Advisory, which are driving high-margin growth and advancing sustainability. Additionally, the company is restructuring and implementing cost reduction initiatives to enhance efficiency and profitability.

evidence.text: Our strategy is driven by our purpose to create a more connected, sustainable world, applying our values and delivering on our brand promise of 'Challenging today. Reinventing tomorrow.' Extensive evaluation of global trends, capabilities and markets identified three growth accelerators: Climate Response, Data Solutions, and Consulting & Advisory, which cut across our entire organization and key sectors creating connections among global market trends, our client solutions and our company purpose. Our three growth accelerators are delivering significant value for our clients, positioning Jacobs for high-margin growth while advancing sustainability and social value in our communities.
evidence.subsection: General

evidence.text: The Company is continually monitoring developments and evaluating the potential impacts [of OECD's Pillar Two Model Rules]. At this time, implementation of these rules has not generated a material impact on consolidated income taxes. The amount of income taxes the Company pays is subject to ongoing audits by tax jurisdictions around the world.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations for the three months ended December 27, 2024 and December 29, 2023

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear long-term strategy with investments in sustainability and innovation for growth.
